title: Create MentoringDbContext and EF Core entity mappings for all 17 tables
description:
## Context
`MentoringApp.Data` already references `Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore` v9.0.12 but uses raw ADO.NET. The full schema (17 tables) is defined as raw SQL in `Data/Repository/SQLite/SqlDbRepo.cs` inside `Recreate()`. All repository interfaces are in `Data/Interfaces/Interfaces.cs`.

## Goal
Create `Data/Repository/Postgres/` with `MentoringDbContext.cs` mapping all 17 tables as typed EF Core entities using the Npgsql provider. The existing SQLite ADO.NET repos must not be touched.

## AI Agent Prompt
In `MentoringApp.Data`:

1. Add `Npgsql.EntityFrameworkCore.PostgreSQL` v9.x to `Data/MentoringApp.Data.csproj`.
2. Create `Data/Repository/Postgres/Entities/` — one POCO class per table. Do NOT reuse the `Model/` domain classes. Map every column from the schema in `SqlDbRepo.cs Recreate()`. Tables: `Users`, `Grades`, `Subjects`, `UserStudents`, `UserMentors`, `UserMentees`, `UserSupervisors`, `UserAdmins`, `SchoolClasses`, `SupervisorClasses`, `VerificationCodes`, `Pairs`, `IssueCategories`, `Issues`, `Reviews`, `PairRequests`, `MatchScores`, `Settings`.
3. On `PairEntity` and `PairRequestEntity` add `public uint RowVersion { get; set; }` mapped with `.UseXminAsConcurrencyToken()` in Fluent API.
4. Create `Data/Repository/Postgres/MentoringDbContext.cs` extending `DbContext` with:
   - `DbContextOptions<MentoringDbContext>` constructor
   - `DbSet<T>` for each entity
   - `OnModelCreating`: all FK relationships, `UNIQUE` constraints (e.g. `SchoolClasses(GradeId, ClassNum)`, `Pairs(MentorId, MenteeId)`), xmin tokens on Pairs/PairRequests, `Settings.Key` as string PK
   - `HasData` seed for 12 Grades rows from `SqlDbRepo.cs`
5. Create `Data/Repository/Postgres/MentoringDbContextFactory.cs` implementing `IDesignTimeDbContextFactory<MentoringDbContext>` with a hardcoded dev connection string so `dotnet ef` CLI works without a startup project.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `Data/Repository/Postgres/` exists with `MentoringDbContext.cs` and `Entities/`
- [ ] All 17 tables mapped; FKs and UNIQUE constraints match SQLite schema
- [ ] `Pairs` and `PairRequests` have xmin concurrency tokens
- [ ] `dotnet build Data/MentoringApp.Data.csproj` passes

title: Implement PgUserRepo and PgVerificationCodeRepo
description:
## Context
`IUserRepo` and `IVerificationCodeRepo` are in `Data/Interfaces/Interfaces.cs`. SQLite counterparts are in `Data/Repository/SQLite/`. DTOs are in `Data/DTO/`. `MentoringDbContext` is created in issue #1.

## Goal
Create `Data/Repository/Postgres/PgUserRepo.cs` implementing `IUserRepo` and `Data/Repository/Postgres/PgVerificationCodeRepo.cs` implementing `IVerificationCodeRepo` using EF Core LINQ — no raw SQL.

## AI Agent Prompt
**PgUserRepo** (implements `IUserRepo`):
- Inject `MentoringDbContext` via constructor.
- `CreateUserAsync`: insert into `Users` + the correct role table (`UserStudents`/`UserMentors`/`UserMentees`/`UserSupervisors`/`UserAdmins`) using pattern matching on the `UserModel` runtime type from `Model/`.
- `GetAllUserDtosAsync`: left-join `Users` with all role tables, project to `UserDto`.
- `UpsertMentorProfileAsync`: delete existing `UserMentors` row for userId if present, then insert fresh.
- `UpdateSupervisorClassesAsync`: delete all `SupervisorClasses` for the supervisor, then bulk insert the new set.
- `GetSupervisorStatisticsAsync`: join `UserSupervisors` + `SupervisorClasses` + `Pairs`, count pairs per supervisor, project to `SupervisorStatsDto`.
- All other methods: straightforward LINQ queries matching interface signatures.

**PgVerificationCodeRepo** (implements `IVerificationCodeRepo`):
- `SaveAsync`: delete existing row for userId, then insert new one.
- `GetUserIdByCodeAsync`: query by code string, return nullable UserId.
- `DeleteAsync`: delete by userId.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Both classes implement their interfaces fully
- [ ] No raw SQL — pure EF Core LINQ
- [ ] `dotnet build Data/MentoringApp.Data.csproj` passes

title: Implement PgPairRepo, PgPairRequestRepo, and PgMatchScoreRepo
description:
## Context
`IPairRepo`, `IPairRequestRepo`, and `IMatchScoreRepo` are in `Data/Interfaces/Interfaces.cs`. The 5-tier matching pipeline in `Service/MatchingFlowService.cs` is the primary consumer. xmin tokens are on `Pairs` and `PairRequests` entities from issue #1.

## Goal
Create the three matching-critical Postgres repo classes in `Data/Repository/Postgres/`.

## AI Agent Prompt
**PgPairRepo** (implements `IPairRepo`):
- Standard LINQ queries projecting to `PairDto` for all Get methods.
- `CreateWithTierAsync`: insert `PairEntity` with `CreatedAt = DateTime.UtcNow.ToString("O")`.
- `GetMatchedMentorIdsAsync` / `GetMatchedMenteeIdsAsync`: return distinct IDs.
- `GetProfileIncompleteAsync`: filter `IsProfileIncomplete == true`.

**PgPairRequestRepo** (implements `IPairRequestRepo`):
- `CreateAsync`: insert with `Status = "Pending"`, `CreatedAt = DateTime.UtcNow.ToString("O")`.
- `UpdateStatusAsync`: find by id, update Status. Catch `DbUpdateConcurrencyException` and re-throw as `InvalidOperationException("Request was modified concurrently — please retry.")`.
- `CancelPendingForUsersAsync`: single `ExecuteUpdateAsync` — set `Status = "Cancelled"` where `MenteeId == menteeId || MentorId == mentorId` and `Status == "Pending"`.
- `ExistsAsync`: return whether a `Pending` row exists for the mentee/mentor pair.
- Add `GetByIdAsync(int requestId)` — also add this method to `IPairRequestRepo` in `Interfaces.cs` and implement a stub in the SQLite repo that throws `NotImplementedException` with a TODO comment.

**PgMatchScoreRepo** (implements `IMatchScoreRepo`):
- `BulkInsertAsync`: `AddRangeAsync` + `SaveChangesAsync`.
- `ClearAllAsync`: `ExecuteDeleteAsync()`.
- `GetTopForMenteeAsync`: order by `ScorePercent DESC`, take limit.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All three interfaces fully implemented
- [ ] `CancelPendingForUsersAsync` uses one `ExecuteUpdateAsync`
- [ ] `IPairRequestRepo` has `GetByIdAsync`
- [ ] `dotnet build Data/MentoringApp.Data.csproj` passes

title: Implement remaining Postgres repos: Issue, Review, Subject, Grade, SchoolClass, Settings, DbRepo
description:
## Context
Remaining interfaces in `Data/Interfaces/Interfaces.cs`: `IIssueRepo`, `IIssueCategoryRepo`, `IReviewRepo`, `ISubjectRepo`, `IGradeRepo`, `ISchoolClassRepo`, `ISettingsRepo`, `IDbRepo`. All straightforward CRUD against `MentoringDbContext`.

## Goal
Create all remaining Postgres repos in `Data/Repository/Postgres/`.

## AI Agent Prompt
**PgIssueRepo**: `GetAllAsync`, `GetByIdAsync`, `GetByReporterAsync`, `GetBySupervisorAsync` — LINQ queries. `CreateAsync` — insert with `IsResolved = false`, `CreationDate = DateTime.UtcNow.ToString("O")`. `ResolveAsync` — find + set `IsResolved = true`. `ForwardAsync` — find + set `ForwardedBySupervisorId`. `GetForwardedAsync` — filter where `ForwardedBySupervisorId IS NOT NULL`.

**PgIssueCategoryRepo**: `GetAllAsync` and `GetByIdAsync` — simple queries.

**PgReviewRepo**: `GetByPairAsync`, `GetByAuthorAsync` — filter + project to `ReviewDto`. `CreateAsync` — insert.

**PgSubjectRepo**: `GetAllSubjectsAsync` — return all rows projected to `SubjectDto`.

**PgGradeRepo**: `GetByIdAsync` and `GetAllGradesAsync` — project to `GradeDto`.

**PgSchoolClassRepo**: `GetAllAsync` — join SchoolClasses+Grades. `GetBySupervisorAsync` — join through SupervisorClasses. `AddAsync` — insert, catch `DbUpdateException` for UNIQUE violation, return false. `DeleteAsync` — delete by id. `SetSupervisorClassesAsync` — in one transaction: delete all existing rows for supervisor, bulk insert new set.

**PgSettingsRepo**: `GetDoubleAsync`/`GetStringAsync` — find by key, parse, return default if not found. `SetDoubleAsync`/`SetStringAsync` — upsert (delete + insert).

**PgDbRepo**: `Recreate()` — call `EnsureDeleted()` then `EnsureCreated()`. Wrap in `#if DEBUG` guard; throw `InvalidOperationException("Recreate() disabled in production.")` otherwise.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All interfaces fully implemented
- [ ] `PgDbRepo.Recreate()` has production guard
- [ ] `dotnet build Data/MentoringApp.Data.csproj` passes

title: Conditional DI: toggle SQLite vs Postgres repos via appsettings DataProvider key
description:
## Context
All repo interfaces are registered in DI in `App.xaml.cs` (WPF). After issues #1–#4, both SQLite and Postgres implementations exist. The API and web projects also need this toggle.

## Goal
Create `Data/DataServiceExtensions.cs` with `AddDataLayer(config)` that reads `DataProvider` from config and registers either SQLite or Postgres repos. Update `App.xaml.cs` (WPF) to use it.

## AI Agent Prompt
1. Add to `MentoringApp/appsettings.json`:
   - `"DataProvider": "sqlite"`
   - `"ConnectionStrings": { ..., "Postgres": "Host=localhost;Port=5432;Database=mentoringapp;Username=postgres;Password=postgres" }`

2. Create `Data/DataServiceExtensions.cs`:
```csharp
public static IServiceCollection AddDataLayer(this IServiceCollection services, IConfiguration config)
{
    return config["DataProvider"]?.ToLower() == "postgres"
        ? services.AddPostgresRepos(config)
        : services.AddSqliteRepos(config);
}
```
`AddSqliteRepos`: move all SQLite repo registrations from `App.xaml.cs` here.
`AddPostgresRepos`: call `services.AddDbContext<MentoringDbContext>(o => o.UseNpgsql(config.GetConnectionString("Postgres")))`, register all `IXxxRepo → PgXxxRepo` bindings as Scoped.

3. In `App.xaml.cs`, replace all individual repo registrations with `services.AddDataLayer(configuration)`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `DataProvider: sqlite` → all `SqlXxxRepo` classes registered
- [ ] `DataProvider: postgres` → all `PgXxxRepo` classes registered
- [ ] `dotnet build MentoringApp.sln` passes with both WPF and the new projects

title: Generate initial EF Core migration and Postgres seed script
description:
## Context
`MentoringDbContext` is ready. The `IDesignTimeDbContextFactory` in issue #1 enables CLI migration generation without a startup project.

## Goal
Generate the `InitialCreate` migration in `Data/Repository/Postgres/Migrations/` and create a `seed.sql` for IssueCategories.

## AI Agent Prompt
From the repo root:
```bash
dotnet ef migrations add InitialCreate \
  --project Data/MentoringApp.Data.csproj \
  --context MentoringDbContext \
  --output-dir Repository/Postgres/Migrations
```

After generation:
1. Review `InitialCreate.cs` — verify all 17 tables, FKs, UNIQUE constraints (`Pairs(MentorId,MenteeId)`, `SchoolClasses(GradeId,ClassNum)`), and xmin tokens are present. Fix any gaps.
2. Create `Data/Repository/Postgres/seed.sql` with `INSERT` statements for `IssueCategories` (same categories as in `DummyDataSeeder.cs`).
3. Create `Data/Repository/Postgres/README.md` with apply instructions:
   ```
   dotnet ef database update --project Data/MentoringApp.Data.csproj --context MentoringDbContext
   psql -d mentoringapp -f Data/Repository/Postgres/seed.sql
   ```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Migration files exist in `Data/Repository/Postgres/Migrations/`
- [ ] Migration applies cleanly against a local Postgres 16 instance
- [ ] `seed.sql` populates IssueCategories
- [ ] `dotnet build Data/MentoringApp.Data.csproj` passes

title: Harden AcceptPairRequestAsync with atomic transaction and optimistic concurrency
Wrap RunAutoMatchAsync and RunFallbackMatchAsync in serializable transactions
description:
## Context
`RunAutoMatchAsync` (line 323, `Service/MatchingFlowService.cs`) uses in-memory HashSets but calls `CreateWithTierAsync` outside any transaction. Concurrent runs can create duplicate pairs. The `Pairs` table has a `UNIQUE(MentorId, MenteeId)` constraint (added in issue #1 migration).

## Goal
Wrap both auto-match methods in serializable transactions. Catch DB-level UNIQUE violations and skip that candidate rather than aborting the entire run.

## AI Agent Prompt
Open `Service/MatchingFlowService.cs`, methods `RunAutoMatchAsync` and `RunFallbackMatchAsync`:

1. Before the `foreach` loop, open: `using var tx = await _context.Database.BeginTransactionAsync(IsolationLevel.Serializable);`
2. Inside the loop, wrap `CreateWithTierAsync` in:
```csharp
try
{
    bool created = await _pairRepo.CreateWithTierAsync(...);
    if (created) { /* track */ pairsCreated++; }
}
catch (DbUpdateException) // UNIQUE constraint violation
{
    continue; // skip this candidate, another process already paired them
}
```
3. After the loop: `await tx.CommitAsync()`.
4. Apply identical pattern to `RunFallbackMatchAsync`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Both methods run inside a serializable transaction
- [ ] Duplicate pair attempts are skipped without crashing the run
- [ ] `dotnet build MentoringApp.sln` passes

title: Create MentoringApp.Api — ASP.NET Core Minimal API project with JWT auth and Swagger
description:
## Context
This is the REST API backend that the Blazor WASM client (and any future mobile/third-party client) will call. It must reference `MentoringApp.Service`, `MentoringApp.Data`, and `MentoringApp.Model`. The WPF project must continue to build independently — no cross-contamination.

## Goal
Scaffold `MentoringApp.Api`, wire all existing DI, implement `/api/auth/login` and `/api/auth/logout` as the first endpoints, configure JWT Bearer auth, and expose Swagger UI in development.

## AI Agent Prompt
1. From repo root:
```bash
dotnet new webapi -n MentoringApp.Api -o MentoringApp.Api --framework net9.0 --use-minimal-apis
dotnet sln MentoringApp.sln add MentoringApp.Api/MentoringApp.Api.csproj
```
2. Add project references to `Service`, `Data`, `Model`.
3. Add NuGet packages: `Microsoft.AspNetCore.Authentication.JwtBearer`, `Swashbuckle.AspNetCore`.
4. In `Program.cs`:
   - Call `builder.Services.AddDataLayer(builder.Configuration)` and `builder.Services.AddServices(builder.Configuration)`.
   - Configure JWT Bearer: read `JwtSettings:Secret`, `Issuer`, `Audience` from `appsettings.json`. Validate issuer + audience + lifetime.
   - Add `builder.Services.AddEndpointsApiExplorer()` and `builder.Services.AddSwaggerGen()` with Bearer token support in the Swagger UI.
   - Add `app.UseSwagger()` and `app.UseSwaggerUI()` inside `if (app.Environment.IsDevelopment())`.
5. Create `MentoringApp.Api/Endpoints/AuthEndpoints.cs`:
   - `POST /api/auth/login` — body: `{ nationalId, password }`. Call `AuthService.LoginAsync(...)`. On success, generate a JWT with claims: `sub = user.Id`, `name = user.UserName`, `role = "Admin"|"Supervisor"|"Student"`. Return `{ token, expiresAt }`.
   - `POST /api/auth/logout` — `[Authorize]` — return `200 OK` (token invalidation is client-side for stateless JWT; document this in a comment).
6. Add `appsettings.json` keys: `"JwtSettings": { "Secret": "CHANGE_ME_32_CHARS_MIN", "Issuer": "MentoringApp", "Audience": "MentoringApp", "ExpiryHours": 8 }`.
7. Map auth endpoints: `app.MapAuthEndpoints()`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `dotnet run --project MentoringApp.Api` starts and Swagger UI is reachable at `/swagger`
- [ ] `POST /api/auth/login` with valid credentials returns a JWT
- [ ] Invalid credentials return `401`
- [ ] `dotnet build MentoringApp.sln` builds all projects including WPF

title: Implement User API endpoints
description:
## Context
`UserService` in `Service/UserService.cs` handles all user CRUD. The API project is scaffolded in issue #8. All endpoints require `[Authorize]`; admin-only endpoints additionally require `Role = "Admin"`.

## Goal
Create `MentoringApp.Api/Endpoints/UserEndpoints.cs` with a full set of user management REST endpoints.

## AI Agent Prompt
Create `MentoringApp.Api/Endpoints/UserEndpoints.cs` as a static class with an extension method `MapUserEndpoints(this WebApplication app)`.

Endpoints (all under `/api/users`):
- `GET /api/users` — `[Authorize(Roles="Admin")]` — returns all users as `IEnumerable<UserDto>`.
- `GET /api/users/{id}` — `[Authorize]` — returns `UserDto` for the given id. Return `404` if not found.
- `POST /api/users` — `[Authorize(Roles="Admin")]` — body: `CreateUserRequest` record with all fields. Call `UserService.CreateUserAsync(...)`. Return `201 Created` with the new user id, or `400` with the `Result.Error` message.
- `DELETE /api/users/{id}` — `[Authorize(Roles="Admin")]` — call `UserService.DeleteUserAsync(id)`. Return `204` or `404`.
- `PUT /api/users/{id}/base-info` — `[Authorize]` — update name, email, nationalId, phone, gender. The logged-in user can only update themselves unless they are Admin.
- `PUT /api/users/{id}/language` — `[Authorize]` — update language preference.
- `GET /api/users/supervisors/stats` — `[Authorize(Roles="Admin")]` — returns `IEnumerable<SupervisorStatsDto>`.

Map in `Program.cs`: `app.MapUserEndpoints()`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All endpoints return correct HTTP status codes
- [ ] Non-admin users cannot call admin-only endpoints (return `403`)
- [ ] Endpoints visible in Swagger UI with correct schemas
- [ ] `dotnet build MentoringApp.Api` passes

title: Implement Pair and Matching API endpoints
description:
## Context
`PairService` and `MatchingFlowService` in `Service/` handle all pair and matching logic. The 5-tier pipeline (Tier 1 requests, Tier 2 score matrix, Tier 3 gallery, Tier 4 auto-match, Tier 5 fallback) must be exposed as REST endpoints.

## Goal
Create `MentoringApp.Api/Endpoints/PairEndpoints.cs` and `MentoringApp.Api/Endpoints/MatchingEndpoints.cs` covering all pair and matching operations.

## AI Agent Prompt
**PairEndpoints** (`/api/pairs`):
- `GET /api/pairs` — `[Authorize(Roles="Admin,Supervisor")]` — all pairs.
- `GET /api/pairs/{id}` — `[Authorize]` — single pair by id.
- `GET /api/pairs/by-mentor/{mentorId}` — `[Authorize]`.
- `GET /api/pairs/by-mentee/{menteeId}` — `[Authorize]`.
- `GET /api/pairs/by-supervisor/{supervisorId}` — `[Authorize(Roles="Admin,Supervisor")]`.
- `POST /api/pairs` — `[Authorize(Roles="Admin")]` — body: `{ supervisorId, mentorId, menteeId }`. Admin manual creation.
- `DELETE /api/pairs/{id}` — `[Authorize(Roles="Admin")]`.

**MatchingEndpoints** (`/api/matching`):
- `GET /api/matching/available-mentors` — `[Authorize]`.
- `GET /api/matching/available-mentees` — `[Authorize]`.
- `POST /api/matching/requests` — `[Authorize(Roles="Student")]` — body: `{ menteeId, mentorId }`. Calls `SendPairRequestAsync`.
- `GET /api/matching/requests/for-mentee/{menteeId}` — `[Authorize]`.
- `GET /api/matching/requests/for-mentor/{mentorId}` — `[Authorize]`.
- `PUT /api/matching/requests/{requestId}/accept` — `[Authorize(Roles="Supervisor,Admin")]` — body: `{ supervisorId }`.
- `PUT /api/matching/requests/{requestId}/reject` — `[Authorize(Roles="Supervisor,Admin")]`.
- `DELETE /api/matching/requests/{requestId}` — `[Authorize(Roles="Student")]` — cancel.
- `GET /api/matching/recommendations/{menteeId}` — `[Authorize]` — top-N gallery recommendations.
- `POST /api/matching/gallery-pick` — `[Authorize(Roles="Student")]` — body: `{ menteeId, mentorId, supervisorId }`.
- `POST /api/matching/pipeline/generate-scores` — `[Authorize(Roles="Admin")]`.
- `POST /api/matching/pipeline/auto-match` — `[Authorize(Roles="Admin")]` — returns `{ pairsCreated: N }`.
- `POST /api/matching/pipeline/fallback-match` — `[Authorize(Roles="Admin")]`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All endpoints return correct status codes and response shapes
- [ ] Role restrictions enforced
- [ ] Endpoints appear in Swagger UI

title: Implement Issue, Review, and reference-data API endpoints
description:
## Context
`IssueService` and `ReviewService` in `Service/`. Reference data endpoints expose Subjects, Grades, and SchoolClasses — required for populating dropdowns in the client.

## Goal
Create `MentoringApp.Api/Endpoints/IssueEndpoints.cs`, `ReviewEndpoints.cs`, and `ReferenceDataEndpoints.cs`.

## AI Agent Prompt
**IssueEndpoints** (`/api/issues`):
- `GET /api/issues` — `[Authorize(Roles="Admin")]` — all issues.
- `GET /api/issues/by-user/{userId}` — `[Authorize]` — issues reported by a user.
- `GET /api/issues/by-supervisor/{supervisorId}` — `[Authorize(Roles="Supervisor,Admin")]`.
- `GET /api/issues/forwarded` — `[Authorize(Roles="Admin")]`.
- `GET /api/issues/categories` — `[Authorize]`.
- `POST /api/issues` — `[Authorize]` — body: `{ description, categoryId, reportedByUserId }`.
- `PUT /api/issues/{id}/resolve` — `[Authorize(Roles="Admin,Supervisor")]`.
- `PUT /api/issues/{id}/forward` — `[Authorize(Roles="Supervisor")]` — body: `{ supervisorId }`.

**ReviewEndpoints** (`/api/reviews`):
- `GET /api/reviews/by-pair/{pairId}` — `[Authorize]`.
- `GET /api/reviews/by-author/{userId}` — `[Authorize]`.
- `POST /api/reviews` — `[Authorize(Roles="Student")]` — body: `{ content, date, pairId, authorUserId, amountOfHours }`.

**ReferenceDataEndpoints** (`/api/reference`):
- `GET /api/reference/subjects` — `[Authorize]`.
- `GET /api/reference/grades` — `[Authorize]`.
- `GET /api/reference/school-classes` — `[Authorize]`.
- `GET /api/reference/school-classes/by-supervisor/{supervisorId}` — `[Authorize]`.

Map all three in `Program.cs`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All endpoints return correct shapes and status codes
- [ ] `dotnet build MentoringApp.Api` passes
- [ ] Full Swagger spec includes all endpoints across all endpoint files

title: Add global error handling, request validation, and CORS to the API
description:
## Context
Minimal API currently has no centralized error handling. Unhandled exceptions will return 500 with a stack trace in development. `Result<T>` is used throughout services for explicit failure signaling — the API must translate these to correct HTTP status codes. CORS must be configured so the Blazor WASM client (a different origin) can call the API.

## Goal
Add a global exception handler middleware, a helper to translate `Result<T>` to `IResult`, input validation via FluentValidation (already in `Service/MentoringApp.Service.csproj`), and a CORS policy for the Blazor client origin.

## AI Agent Prompt
In `MentoringApp.Api`:

1. Add `app.UseExceptionHandler("/error")` to the middleware pipeline. Map `GET /error` as a minimal endpoint that returns `Results.Problem(...)` from `IExceptionHandlerFeature`. In development, include the exception message; in production, use a generic message.

2. Create `MentoringApp.Api/Helpers/ResultExtensions.cs`:
```csharp
public static IResult ToHttpResult<T>(this Result<T> result)
    => result.Success ? Results.Ok(result.Data) : Results.BadRequest(new { error = result.Error });

public static IResult ToHttpResult(this Result result)
    => result.Success ? Results.Ok() : Results.BadRequest(new { error = result.Error });
```
Replace raw `Results.Ok(...)` / `Results.BadRequest(...)` calls in all endpoint files with `.ToHttpResult()`.

3. In `Program.cs` add CORS:
```csharp
builder.Services.AddCors(o => o.AddPolicy("BlazorClient", p =>
    p.WithOrigins(builder.Configuration["AllowedOrigins"] ?? "http://localhost:5173")
     .AllowAnyMethod()
     .AllowAnyHeader()));
app.UseCors("BlazorClient");
```
Add `"AllowedOrigins": "http://localhost:5173"` to `appsettings.Development.json`.

4. Add `SharpGrip.FluentValidation.AutoValidation.Minimal` or manually validate request records at the start of each endpoint using `UserValidator` (already exists in `Service/Validator/`).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Unhandled exceptions return `application/problem+json` not a raw stack trace
- [ ] `Result.Failure` translates to `400 Bad Request` with `{ error: "..." }`
- [ ] Blazor WASM origin can call the API without CORS errors
- [ ] `dotnet build MentoringApp.Api` passes

title: Create MentoringApp.Web Blazor WebAssembly project with typed HTTP clients and JWT auth
description:
## Context
The REST API is ready. The Blazor WASM client runs in the browser and communicates exclusively via HTTP — it has no direct reference to `Service` or `Data` projects. JWT tokens returned by `POST /api/auth/login` must be stored in memory (not `localStorage`) and attached as `Authorization: Bearer` headers on every request.

## Goal
Scaffold `MentoringApp.Web` as a standalone Blazor WebAssembly project. Create one typed `HttpClient` wrapper per API domain group. Implement the login page and JWT storage service.

## AI Agent Prompt
1. From repo root:
```bash
dotnet new blazorwasm -n MentoringApp.Web -o MentoringApp.Web --framework net9.0
dotnet sln MentoringApp.sln add MentoringApp.Web/MentoringApp.Web.csproj
```
Do NOT add project references to `Service`, `Data`, or `Model` — the client communicates only via HTTP.

2. Add NuGet: `Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.WebAssembly.Authentication`.

3. In `Program.cs`:
   - Register a base `HttpClient` pointing at the API base URL from `appsettings.json` (`"ApiBaseUrl": "https://localhost:7001"`).
   - Create typed clients: `AuthApiClient`, `UserApiClient`, `PairApiClient`, `MatchingApiClient`, `IssueApiClient`, `ReviewApiClient`, `ReferenceApiClient` — each wrapping an `HttpClient` and exposing strongly-typed async methods matching the API endpoints. Register them with `builder.Services.AddHttpClient<T>`.

4. Create `MentoringApp.Web/Services/TokenService.cs` — scoped service:
   - `string? Token` property (in-memory only — no localStorage)
   - `SetToken(string token)` / `ClearToken()`
   - `IsAuthenticated` property
   - `GetUserClaims()` — parse JWT payload (base64 decode the middle segment)

5. Create a `JwtAuthorizationMessageHandler` (implements `DelegatingHandler`) that reads `TokenService.Token` and sets `Authorization: Bearer {token}` on every outbound request. Register it with all typed clients.

6. Create `MentoringApp.Web/Pages/Login.razor` at route `/login`:
   - Fields: NationalId, Password
   - On submit: call `AuthApiClient.LoginAsync(...)`, on success call `TokenService.SetToken(token)` and navigate to the appropriate dashboard based on the `role` claim.
   - Show error message on failure.

7. Create `MentoringApp.Web/Services/RouteGuard.razor` — a component that checks `TokenService.IsAuthenticated` on `OnInitializedAsync` and redirects to `/login` if false. Wrap `<RouteGuard>` around the `<Router>` in `App.razor`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `dotnet run --project MentoringApp.Web` starts and serves the login page
- [ ] Successful login stores the token and redirects
- [ ] All API calls include the Bearer token header
- [ ] Unauthenticated navigation to any page redirects to `/login`

title: Build MainLayout with RTL/LTR toggle and navigation shell
description:
## Context
The app supports Hebrew (RTL) and English (LTR). The user's language preference is stored in the JWT `language` claim (or fetched from `GET /api/users/{id}`). RTL is handled via CSS `direction: rtl` and `lang="he"` on the root element. No `TranslationSource` singleton — the web client uses `IStringLocalizer<T>` with the existing `.resx` files.

## Goal
Create `MentoringApp.Web/Shared/MainLayout.razor` with a role-aware sidebar nav, a language toggle button, and CSS-driven RTL/LTR support.

## AI Agent Prompt
In `MentoringApp.Web`:

1. Copy `MentoringApp/Localization/Strings.resx` and `Strings.he.resx` into `MentoringApp.Web/Localization/`. In `Program.cs` add `builder.Services.AddLocalization(o => o.ResourcesPath = "Localization")`.

2. Create `MentoringApp.Web/Services/LayoutStateService.cs` — a singleton (for the WASM session lifetime) holding `string Language` and exposing `event Action? OnChange`. The language toggle updates this service and calls `OnChange`.

3. Create `MentoringApp.Web/Shared/MainLayout.razor`:
   - Renders `<div dir="@_dir" lang="@_lang">` wrapping `@Body`.
   - Sidebar links are role-conditional: Admin sees Users + Matching Pipeline; Supervisor sees their dashboard; Student sees Profile + Matching + Issues + Reviews.
   - Language toggle button calls `UserApiClient.UpdateLanguageAsync(userId, lang)` and updates `LayoutStateService`.
   - Logout button calls `TokenService.ClearToken()` and navigates to `/login`.

4. `MentoringApp.Web/wwwroot/css/app.css`:
```css
[dir="rtl"] { text-align: right; }
[dir="rtl"] .sidebar { left: auto; right: 0; }
[dir="rtl"] .nav-item { flex-direction: row-reverse; }
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Hebrew users see RTL layout; English users see LTR
- [ ] Language toggle persists to API and reflects immediately without page reload
- [ ] Sidebar shows only role-appropriate links

title: Admin Dashboard: user management table
description:
## Context
`UserApiClient` calls `GET /api/users`, `POST /api/users`, `DELETE /api/users/{id}`. The WPF equivalent is `AdminDashboardView.xaml` and `AdminDashboardViewModel.cs`.

## Goal
Create `MentoringApp.Web/Pages/Admin/AdminDashboard.razor` with a searchable user table and create/delete actions.

## AI Agent Prompt
Create `MentoringApp.Web/Pages/Admin/AdminDashboard.razor` at route `/admin`:

1. `OnInitializedAsync`: call `UserApiClient.GetAllUsersAsync()`, store in `_users`.
2. Search `<input>` — filters displayed rows client-side by Name or NationalId.
3. Table columns: Name, NationalId, Email, Role, Actions.
4. Delete button: confirmation `<dialog>`, then `UserApiClient.DeleteUserAsync(id)`, refresh list.
5. "Create User" toggle button: inline form with fields: UserName, Email, NationalId, Phone, Gender (dropdown from `Gender` enum values), Role (Admin/Supervisor/Student). On submit: `UserApiClient.CreateUserAsync(request)`. Show `Result.Error` inline on failure. On success: close form, prepend new user to list.
6. Show a loading spinner (`_isLoading` bool) while any async call is in flight.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Table shows all users with correct role label
- [ ] Search filters without a server round-trip
- [ ] Create and delete work end-to-end through the API
- [ ] Delete requires confirmation

title: Admin Matching Pipeline page: score matrix, auto-match, and fallback controls
description:
## Context
`MatchingApiClient` calls: `POST /api/matching/pipeline/generate-scores`, `POST /api/matching/pipeline/auto-match` (returns `{ pairsCreated }`), `POST /api/matching/pipeline/fallback-match`. Pair list: `GET /api/pairs`.

## Goal
Create `MentoringApp.Web/Pages/Admin/MatchingPipeline.razor` with step-by-step pipeline controls and the current pair list.

## AI Agent Prompt
Create `MentoringApp.Web/Pages/Admin/MatchingPipeline.razor` at route `/admin/matching`:

1. Three action cards in a row:
   - **Generate Scores** → `MatchingApiClient.GenerateScoresAsync()`. Show success/failure message.
   - **Auto-Match** → `MatchingApiClient.RunAutoMatchAsync()`. Show `"X pairs created"`.
   - **Fallback Match** → `MatchingApiClient.RunFallbackMatchAsync()`. Show count.
2. Each button disables while in-flight (`_isRunningN` bool per card).
3. Pair table below: Mentor, Mentee, Supervisor, Tier (human-readable: Direct/Gallery/AutoMatch/Fallback/Random), Date, incomplete-profile badge (red).
4. Rows with `IsProfileIncomplete = true` get a yellow background.
5. Delete pair button with confirmation. Calls `PairApiClient.DeletePairAsync(id)`. Refreshes table.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All three pipeline steps callable and show feedback
- [ ] Table refreshes after each step
- [ ] Incomplete-profile pairs visually distinct

title: Supervisor Dashboard: pair monitoring and issue queue
description:
## Context
`PairApiClient.GetBySupervisorAsync(id)`, `IssueApiClient.GetBySupervisorAsync(id)`, `IssueApiClient.ResolveAsync(id)`, `IssueApiClient.ForwardAsync(id, supervisorId)`. Supervisor ID from JWT claim.

## Goal
Create `MentoringApp.Web/Pages/Supervisor/SupervisorDashboard.razor`.

## AI Agent Prompt
Create `MentoringApp.Web/Pages/Supervisor/SupervisorDashboard.razor` at route `/supervisor`:

1. Load supervisor's pairs and issues on init.
2. **Pairs section**: table — Mentor, Mentee, Tier, Date, Incomplete badge.
3. **Issues section**: table — Description (truncated), Category, Reporter, Date, Status badge (green Resolved / orange Open), Forwarded badge.
   - Resolve button: `IssueApiClient.ResolveAsync(id)`. Refresh.
   - Forward button: `IssueApiClient.ForwardAsync(id, supervisorId)`. Disable if already forwarded.
4. Issues section header badge: `"Issues (N unresolved)"`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Shows only data for the logged-in supervisor
- [ ] Resolve and Forward update correctly
- [ ] Already-forwarded issues disable the Forward button

title: Student Profile page: edit base info and mentor/mentee settings
description:
## Context
`UserApiClient` provides `UpdateBaseInfoAsync`, `UpdateLanguageAsync`. Separate endpoints (added in issue #10 or as needed) cover grade/class, gender preferences, and mentor profile upsert. Reference data comes from `ReferenceApiClient`. The student's current data is in the JWT claims plus a `GET /api/users/{id}` call.

## Goal
Create `MentoringApp.Web/Pages/Student/Profile.razor` with independently saveable sections.

## AI Agent Prompt
Create `MentoringApp.Web/Pages/Student/Profile.razor` at route `/student/profile`:

1. Load full user via `UserApiClient.GetByIdAsync(userId)`, grades via `ReferenceApiClient.GetGradesAsync()`, subjects via `ReferenceApiClient.GetSubjectsAsync()`.
2. **Base info**: UserName, Email, Phone, Gender. Save → `UserApiClient.UpdateBaseInfoAsync(...)`.
3. **Grade & Class**: Grade dropdown + class number. Save → `UserApiClient.UpdateGradeClassAsync(...)`.
4. **Gender preferences**: preferred mentor gender + preferred mentee gender. Save → `UserApiClient.UpdateGenderPreferencesAsync(...)`.
5. **Mentor profile** (only if `isMentor` claim is true): Subject dropdown, MaxMentees number input. Save → `UserApiClient.UpsertMentorProfileAsync(...)`.
6. **Mentee profile** (only if `isMentee` claim): Subject dropdown. Save → `UserApiClient.UpdateMenteeProfileAsync(...)`.
7. Success toast per section — auto-dismisses after 3 seconds.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Each section saves independently
- [ ] Mentor/mentee sections render only when applicable
- [ ] Dropdowns populated from API

title: Matching Flow: direct pair requests (Tier 1) and gallery picker (Tier 3)
description:
## Context
`MatchingApiClient` covers Tier 1 (send/accept/reject/cancel requests) and Tier 3 (get recommendations, gallery-pick). The WPF equivalents are in the student dashboard. These two flows are closely related and share the same page with conditional rendering based on the student's current state.

## Goal
Create `MentoringApp.Web/Pages/Student/Matching.razor` combining both flows. Show the gallery if recommendations exist, fall through to the direct-request list if not.

## AI Agent Prompt
Create `MentoringApp.Web/Pages/Student/Matching.razor` at route `/student/matching`:

**If the student is already paired**: show their pair info and a link to the Reviews page. Skip all matching UI.

**Tier 3 — Gallery** (shown first if recommendations exist):
1. Call `MatchingApiClient.GetRecommendationsAsync(menteeId, topN: 3)`.
2. Render top-N mentor cards: Name, Gender icon, Subject, compatibility score as a progress bar (`ScorePercent` 0–100).
3. "Choose" button → `MatchingApiClient.GalleryPickAsync(...)`. On success: show confirmation banner, hide gallery. On failure: show error, refresh cards.
4. "Skip gallery" link scrolls to the Tier 1 section below.

**Tier 1 — Direct Requests**:
5. List available mentors via `MatchingApiClient.GetAvailableMentorsAsync()`. Table: Name, Gender, Subject, capacity indicator.
6. "Send Request" per mentor → `MatchingApiClient.SendPairRequestAsync(menteeId, mentorId)`. Disable button if pending request exists for that mentor.
7. "My pending requests" section: list with mentor name + "Cancel" button per entry.

**Mentor view** (shown if `isMentor` claim, below mentee sections):
8. Incoming requests table: Mentee Name, Subject, Date, Accept/Reject buttons.
9. Accept → `MatchingApiClient.AcceptPairRequestAsync(requestId, supervisorId)`. Show concurrency error if returned.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Already-matched students see their pair, not the matching UI
- [ ] Gallery cards show accurate scores
- [ ] Direct requests can be sent, viewed, and cancelled
- [ ] Mentor can accept and reject requests; concurrency error shown clearly

title: Issue reporting and review submission pages
description:
## Context
`IssueApiClient` and `ReviewApiClient` are typed clients wrapping the issue and review endpoints. Both pages are accessible to students. Issue submission is append-only. Reviews require an active pair.

## Goal
Create `MentoringApp.Web/Pages/User/Issues.razor` and `MentoringApp.Web/Pages/Student/Reviews.razor`.

## AI Agent Prompt
**Issues.razor** at route `/issues` (`[Authorize]`):
1. Load categories via `IssueApiClient.GetCategoriesAsync()` and user's issues via `IssueApiClient.GetByUserAsync(userId)`.
2. Collapsible "Report New Issue" form: textarea (min 10 chars), category dropdown. Submit → `IssueApiClient.CreateAsync(...)`. Prepend to list on success.
3. Issue list with: category filter dropdown + status filter (All/Open/Resolved) — client-side filtering. Columns: Description (80 char truncation), Category, Date, Status badge, Forwarded badge.
4. Inline validation: empty description or no category blocks submission.

**Reviews.razor** at route `/student/reviews` (`[Authorize(Roles="Student")]`):
1. Load student's pair via `PairApiClient.GetByMentorOrMenteeAsync(userId)`. If none: show holding message.
2. Load pair history via `ReviewApiClient.GetByPairAsync(pairId)`. Show as timeline: date, author, hours, content. Total hours at top.
3. Submit form: textarea, hours input (0.5 step, 0.5–8), date picker (default today). Submit → `ReviewApiClient.CreateAsync(...)`. Prepend to timeline, update total.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Issues: submit creates and appears immediately; filters work client-side
- [ ] Reviews: unpaired students see holding message; hours total recalculates after submit
- [ ] Both pages accessible from sidebar

title: Dockerize: Dockerfile for API, static host for WASM, and docker-compose
description:
## Context
The solution now has two runnable projects: `MentoringApp.Api` (ASP.NET Core) and `MentoringApp.Web` (Blazor WASM — static files served by either the API or a dedicated nginx). Secrets (SMTP password, JWT secret) must not appear in tracked files.

## Goal
Create `Dockerfile` for the API (which also serves the WASM static files), a `docker-compose.yml` for local dev (app + Postgres), and an `.env.example` documenting all secrets.

## AI Agent Prompt
1. Configure the API to serve the Blazor WASM static files: in `MentoringApp.Api.csproj` add a project reference to `MentoringApp.Web`. In `Program.cs` add `app.UseBlazorFrameworkFiles(); app.UseStaticFiles(); app.MapFallbackToFile("index.html");`. This is the "hosted Blazor WASM" pattern.

2. Create `Dockerfile` at repo root:
```dockerfile
FROM mcr.microsoft.com/dotnet/sdk:9.0 AS build
WORKDIR /src
COPY . .
RUN dotnet publish MentoringApp.Api/MentoringApp.Api.csproj -c Release -o /app/publish

FROM mcr.microsoft.com/dotnet/aspnet:9.0 AS final
WORKDIR /app
COPY --from=build /app/publish .
ENTRYPOINT ["dotnet", "MentoringApp.Api.dll"]
```

3. Create `docker-compose.yml`:
   - `db`: `postgres:16-alpine`, volume `pgdata`, env `POSTGRES_DB=mentoringapp POSTGRES_USER=postgres POSTGRES_PASSWORD=postgres`, port `5432:5432`.
   - `api`: build from `./Dockerfile`, depends_on `db`, port `8080:8080`. Environment:
     - `DataProvider=postgres`
     - `ConnectionStrings__Postgres=Host=db;Port=5432;Database=mentoringapp;Username=postgres;Password=postgres`
     - `JwtSettings__Secret`, `EmailSettings__SmtpHost`, `EmailSettings__FromEmail`, `EmailSettings__FromPassword` — sourced from `.env` file.

4. Create `.env.example` at repo root with all required variables as empty placeholders and comments.
5. Ensure `.env` is in `.gitignore`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `docker compose up` starts both services
- [ ] API reachable at `http://localhost:8080/swagger`
- [ ] Blazor WASM served at `http://localhost:8080`
- [ ] No secrets in tracked files

title: GitLab CI/CD: build, test, migrate, and deploy pipeline
description:
## Context
The solution has 6 projects. Two are runnable: `MentoringApp.Api` and `MentoringApp.Web`. Migrations must be applied to Postgres before the new version goes live. Secrets are in GitLab CI/CD variables.

## Goal
Create `.gitlab-ci.yml` with stages: `build`, `migrate`, `deploy`. Push to `main` runs all three; push to `develop` runs only `build`.

## AI Agent Prompt
Create `.gitlab-ci.yml` at repo root:

```yaml
stages:
  - build
  - migrate
  - deploy

build:
  stage: build
  image: mcr.microsoft.com/dotnet/sdk:9.0
  script:
    - dotnet restore MentoringApp.sln
    - dotnet build MentoringApp.sln -c Release --no-restore
    - dotnet test Tests/MentoringApp.Tests.csproj --no-build -c Release || true
    - dotnet publish MentoringApp.Api/MentoringApp.Api.csproj -c Release -o publish/
  artifacts:
    paths: [publish/]
    expire_in: 1 hour
  only: [main, develop]

migrate:
  stage: migrate
  image: mcr.microsoft.com/dotnet/sdk:9.0
  variables:
    ConnectionStrings__Postgres: $PROD_POSTGRES_CONNECTION
  script:
    - dotnet tool install --global dotnet-ef
    - export PATH="$PATH:$HOME/.dotnet/tools"
    - dotnet ef database update
        --project Data/MentoringApp.Data.csproj
        --context MentoringDbContext
  only: [main]

deploy:
  stage: deploy
  needs: [migrate]
  script:
    # Replace with: ssh deploy, kubectl apply, docker stack deploy, etc.
    - echo "Deploy publish/ to production — configure for your infra"
  only: [main]
```

Also:
1. Add `.dockerignore` excluding `obj/`, `bin/`, `*.db`, `.env`, `MentoringApp/` (WPF — not needed in container).
2. Document required CI/CD variables in `docs/deployment.md`: `PROD_POSTGRES_CONNECTION`, `JwtSettings__Secret`, `EmailSettings__FromEmail`, `EmailSettings__FromPassword`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Pipeline triggers on push to `main` and `develop`
- [ ] Build fails fast if `dotnet build` fails
- [ ] Migrate runs only on `main` using the CI/CD variable — no hardcoded connection string
- [ ] `.gitlab-ci.yml` contains no secrets
